---
title: "About"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: profile.png          # add a headshot here, or delete this line
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/vikramdixit"
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: "https://github.com/VikkiD"
    - icon: file-earmark-text
      text: Résumé
      href: resume.pdf
    - icon: rss
      text: Subscribe
      href: index.xml
---

I've spent several years doing research, writing code, and thinking about
questions of human choice and decision making. Where I've studied and worked is
in my [résumé](resume.pdf) --- this space is for the thinking.

## Why this, why now?

I read a lot of posts and keep catching myself asking the same thing: *why
should I trust this?* What has the writer actually done, and what analysis stands
behind the claim? That question is a large part of why I started writing. I'd
rather show the work --- the method, the data, the reasoning --- than hand over a
tidy takeaway and ask you to take my word for it.

I also learn better when I have to explain something to someone other than
myself. So this is partly selfish: writing carefully is how I come to understand
things properly.

## What is this about?

A space for the curious --- people looking for answers to questions we have all
run into that don't quite scratch the itch, who want the finer details of the
methods and the results, not just the headline.

## What to expect

A post most Fridays, on a topic in economics, econometrics, statistics, machine
learning, artificial intelligence, or computer science. Each one is either a
description of a method --- how to apply it, when to apply it, why it matters now
--- or an analysis of a real question worked through with data. I try to make
them worth reading: rigorous, but not a slog. Everything is free.
